title: Jedi Tower
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Jedi Academy}} {{Structure |image=[[File:JediTempleOnTaris-KOTOR1.jpg]] |name=Jedi Tower |constructed=–c. [[3977 BBY]] The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' briefly describes the teaching arrangements at the Tower "''in the years immediately before the Mandalorian Wars''." Year in the plural implies at least 2 years, and the start of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] is given to be [[3976 BBY]] in the Campaign Guide. Logic dictates that the Tower must have been constructed at least 2 years before this. Any year ''prior to'' [[3977 BBY]] would be at least 2 years before 3976 BBY, hence the date of construction is put as "–c. 3977 BBY". |destroyed=[[3963 BBY]][[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]] |type=Tower[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]] |location=[[Highport]], [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]{{Faraway|subdomain=blog|url=2010/02/kotor-coundown-9-just-asked-questions.html|text=KOTOR Countdown #9: Just-Asked Questions, Part One}} |interest= |rebuilt= |builder= |height= |width= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] }} {{Quote|For five years, this building was home.|Zayne Carrick|}} The '''Jedi Tower''', also referred to as the '''Taris Academy''', was a [[Jedi outpost/Legends|Jedi outpost]] and [[Jedi academy|satellite academy]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] built in [[Highport]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. It was used by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] prior to and during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], a conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Isolated from other structures by a skybridge, the Tower was a tall, tapering building with the [[Jedi Council/Legends#Taris|Council]] chamber at its pinnacle. Adjoining the main structure was a smaller, vertical tower that contained a [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] to access different levels of the Tower via walkways between the two. Surrounding the base of the Tower was a [[Hangar/Legends|landing area]] staffed by [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]]. In [[3964 BBY]], the Tower was operated by the [[First WatchCircle]] of the [[Jedi Covenant]] who [[Death/Legends|killed]] their students in the [[Padawan Massacre]] of Taris. Nevertheless, one of the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]]—a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Zayne Carrick]]—escaped the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]]' trap and was forced to flee offworld with the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[Marn Hierogryph]] after both were framed as the culprits of the Massacre. The Masters were recalled to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when their efforts to track Carrick down failed, with the Padawan even contacting them to say that he would track down and kill each Master until his name was cleared. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], led by [[Cassus Fett]], later [[Taris Siege|invaded]] the planet and took the Tower as their own. Carrick returned to Taris to assist Hierogryph and the [[Taris Resistance]] in their plans to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Fett by blowing the Tower up with the Mandalorian inside. The [[Togruta/Legends|Togrutan]] [[Raana Tey]], one of the First WatchCircle and still wanting Carrick dead, provisionally cooperated with the former Padawan and [[Shel Jelavan]] in their planned role of infiltrating the Tower prior to detonation to determine if Fett was still inside. Simultaneously, however, the paranoid Tey intended to kill Carrick before he could kill her as he had warned. When the plan was [[Mission to the Jedi Tower|enacted]], Carrick discovered that the Mandalorian leader was not in the Tower, and was confronted by Tey. They [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueled]] until Jelavan intervened, wounding Tey with her [[Shad Jelavan|brother's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. [[Gadon Thek]], a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] leader helping the Resistance, and Hierogryph arrived to evacuate them on Thek's [[Swoop/Legends|swoop bike]], but Tey became stuck and used her lightsaber to free herself. Perceiving this as a threat, the Snivvian prematurely detonated preset explosives, and Tey was killed in the destruction of the Tower. ==Description== [[File:Jedi_Tower_Skywalk.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Tower's skywalk]] The Jedi Tower was located in [[Highport]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The foundation of the Tower was part of a disused [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] control complex that once recycled the polluted air in the deeper Tarisian streets to remove the contaminants. A ventilation shaft ran vertically down from the storage level of the Tower into the foundations, where other shafts opened out to the exterior and the [[Lower City]]. The atmosphere control complex was set away from the nearby residential buildings and other structures, necessitating the construction of a skybridge for access. The skybridge, which spanned the gap between a Highport plaza and the base of the Tower, was wide enough to later accommodate a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] cannon and was adorned by a colonnade consisting of six individual columns. Those using the skybridge arrived at an entrance to the Tower that went below the [[Hangar/Legends|landing area]] above. On the floor above the skybridge entrance was the landing area where [[Airbus/Legends|shuttlebuses]] and [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] landed. The apron was staffed by [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]] to assist in the loading and unloading of cargo.[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] At night, the landing area was illuminated by red, conical lights.[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] A [[Garden/Legends|garden]] plaza was located on the same level. Several bays for keeping vessels in were arrayed below the Tower with one of these leading through to a doorway to the storage area inside the Tower base.[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] [[File:Taris_Jedi_Tower_Storage_Level.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The storage room in the Tower]] The storage area was normally staffed by [[LB-series bulk-loading droid]]s coordinating the supplies for the hangars below and had three open shafts leading down to the landing area.{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?t=8847&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=15|thread=KNIGHTS of the OLD REPUBLIC #2 -- Run, Zayne, Run!|user=[[John Jackson Miller]]|time=06:35}} [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] played hiding games in the room, packed with crates of varying sizes. An access hatch to the ventilation shaft from the foundations was set in the floor of the storage room and was kept locked. At the back of the room was a [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] which ran all the way up a slim, vertical tower that was connected to the tower-proper by five walkways. A spire was installed atop the turbolift tower and shone a red beacon from its tip at night. There was a room used to brief students on their assignments that was equipped with a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] in the main tower.[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]] A [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] room used by the [[Jedi Seer|seers]] of the [[First WatchCircle]] had access from one of the walkways off the turbolift tower.[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] The very top of the main tower was occupied by the Council chamber. It was similar in layout to its [[Jedi Enclave|counterpart]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], though it lacked [[Window/Legends|windows]] and had only five chairs. The walls had a gray-and-red design built into it, and the chairs were adorned with a similar color scheme. The ceiling was made of [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] and allowed light in during the day. [[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsabers]] were not permitted to be worn while in the Council chambers except on special occasions such as [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|Knighting ceremonies]]. A pair of racks for stowing lightsabers sat to the side of the room. After the Council had withdrawn from Taris, the Mandalorians were able to set up their base of operations for their [[Taris Siege|takeover]] of the planet in the Council chamber. Tactical [[Hologram/Legends|holograms]] were projected on the floor, and the Council's seats were taken up by the nomads' equipment. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|If the number of Knights on Taris were to double overnight, it wouldn't trouble me in the least.|Sector Constable [[Noana Sowrs]]|{{KOTORbackups|0}}}} [[File:Jedi_Covenant_NEGTF.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Jedi Tower Council]] Before the [[Mandalorian Wars]], a war fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]], the classes at the Jedi Tower varied in size. Shortly before the outbreak of the war, the Tower's [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]] included [[Feln]], a [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin]]; [[Q'Anilia]], a [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]]; [[Raana Tey]], a [[Togruta/Legends|Togrutan]]; [[Xamar]], a [[Khil]]; and the head of the Academy, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Lucien Draay]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' They were also the members of the First WatchCircle, a branch of the secret [[Jedi Covenant]], and used the Tower as their base of operations while they watched for the rise of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] to prevent it from unfolding. Draay's position as a teacher at the [[Jedi academy]] was used as a cover for his work in the WatchCircle.[[Knights of the Old Republic 33|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 33]] He was also the only one of the five Masters stationed at the Tower not to be a [[Jedi Consular]] like the others. It was unusual for an academy to have so many seers in one place because of the shortage in candidates, something that Masters [[Vandar Tokare]] and [[Vrook Lamar]] were suspicious of.[[Knights of the Old Republic 9|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 9]] In the years immediately before the Mandalorian Wars, each of the five Masters at the Tower had had their own student. [[Shad Jelavan]] was a Human apprenticed to Q'Anilia and was native to Taris; [[Gharn]], a [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]], was Xamar's Padawan; the [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Oojoh]] was the Padawan of Feln; Tey's apprentice was [[Kamlin]], a [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]]; and Draay was Master to [[Zayne Carrick]], a Human. Aside from the Jedi, the Tower was occupied by many loading [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to facilitate management of the logistics of the Tower. ==History== ===Early teaching=== The Jedi Tower was a [[Jedi outpost/Legends|Jedi outpost]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' that was built before the Mandalorian Wars in Highport on Taris, a then-unaffiliated planet in the Outer Rim Territories of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Prior to the outbreak of the war, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] bought the Tower to set up a Jedi academy, choosing the building due to its isolation from residential blocks. From [[3964 BBY]], the students aided the [[Taris Civil Authority|local law enforcement]] with day-to-day policing, and although there were only a few Jedi on the planet, their presence was appreciated by the Tarisian public.{{Faraway|subdomain=blog|url=2010/02/kotor-countdown-3-just-asked-questions.html|text=KOTOR Countdown #3: Just-Asked Questions, Part Four}} Successful assignments undertaken by some of the Padawans included breaking up of the [[Leverby smuggling ring]] by Oojoh and the capture of [[P'den Robalt]] by Kamlin. Seven times Carrick tried—and failed—to capture the [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[black marketeer]], [[Marn Hierogryph]].Hierogryph says in [[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Commencement, Part 2'']] that Carrick has tried to capture him nine times. The ninth time was successful and near the end of [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Commencement, Part 1'']], with the eighth time being earlier in this issue. This makes the attempt in [[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Crossroads'']] the seventh time. ===The Padawan Massacre=== {{Quote|I didn't do anything! I went into the Temple and found all my friends dead!''"
"''Dead?''"
"''''Murdered!'' Their Masters killed them! Their own Masters! And now they're after us!|Zayne Carrick and Marn Hierogryph, after evading the Council|}} Over a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] later,In [[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Crossroads'']] Carrick says: "My class has a challenge to face [[Rogue Moon|off-world]] next week." The events on the Rogue moon took place before Carrick's eighth attempt in [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Commencement, Part 1'']]. Carrick was on the tail of Hierogryph again and managed to detain the criminal in the second of two attempts. The Padawan took him back to the Jedi Tower and, as Carrick was late for his Knighting ceremony, took the turbolift up to the Council chambers after locking Hierogryph to his [[Urban Navigator|speeder]]. [[File:GryphWhatDidYouDo.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Carrick flees his Masters.]] In the Council chambers above, the other Padawans were arguing with the Masters over the possibility of Carrick being Knighted, not that they wouldn't have been happy for him. The Padawans found this sudden turnaround of the Masters' opinion of Carrick to be very suspicious. After failing to convince the students of their reasons, the Masters acted on a [[Rogue Moon Prophecy|vision]] they received earlier and [[Padawan Massacre|killed them]]. It was at this moment that Carrick entered the room, and seeing his friends [[Death/Legends|dead]], he turned and ran back to the turbolift with the Masters in pursuit. Carrick used his lightsaber to cut through a pair of doors in the turbolift shaft and emerged on the Tower's storage level before jumping down another chute that brought him out onto the hangar level. The fleeing Padawan started his speeder, with Hierogryph still locked to it, and retraced his route back up the turbolift shaft, smashing through a window in the walkway to the Council chambers to escape. Despite giving chase on [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]], the Masters failed to find the two fugitives and returned to the Tower where the dead Padawans' bodies were being removed from the Council chamber by [[Droid/Legends|coroner droids]]. Meanwhile, Hierogryph and Carrick made their way off-world with help from an [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|offshoot]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], [[Gorman Vandrayk]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]] Despite evading an attempt by Draay to detain them on the [[Rogue Moon|Rogue moon]], they were captured by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Valius Ying]]. Carrick agreed to turn himself in to the Jedi so that his companions could go free.[[Knights of the Old Republic 5|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 5]] Ying took the Padawan back down to Taris and brought him before the Council in the Jedi Tower. Master Draay retold the events leading up to the Massacre and explained that they were members of the Jedi Covenant. Draay then killed Ying to prevent the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter from retelling what he had heard and prepared to attack his former Padawan. At that moment, the whole room shook, and the power failed as the Tower was fired upon from above. Vandrayk's companion, [[Jarael]], crashed through the skylight and absconded with Carrick through the broken skylight, landing on the top of the uppermost walkway. They climbed to the end of the walkway where they escaped aboard the waiting [[The Last Resort (starship)|''Last Resort'']]. Three weeks later, the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Dantooine Council]] ordered the Tower Council back to Coruscant, deeming their failure to capture Carrick as damaging public respect for the Jedi's authority. At this time, Carrick sent a message to the Masters in which he said that he had foreseen one of the Masters confessing to what they had done. He vowed that he would track down those who did not confess and that those Masters would die. ===In Mandalorian hands=== {{Quote|We've just learned that ''Cassus Fett'' himself has come to review the operations from Highport. He's using your Jedi Tower as his command center.|Haydel Goravvus, to Raana Tey|}} [[File:MandalorianJediTower-KotOR23.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Jedi Tower flying Neo-Crusader colors]] After the Jedi left Taris, the Republic forces protecting it were diverted to defend [[Vanquo]] against the incoming Mandalorian forces.[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] This left Taris wide open to attack and, in [[3963 BBY]], Mandalorian [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]] [[Cassus Fett]] led the invasion of the planet.{{KOTORbackups|18}} Fett took the empty Jedi Tower as his command center by setting up tactical holograms in the now-candlelit Council chambers so that he had the ability to manage the traffic flow of the invasion force.''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' A Neo-Crusader banner was flown from the mast on top of the turbolift tower, and a Mandalorian artillery cannon was installed on the skybridge. Meanwhile, in the Lower City of the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]], the [[Taris Resistance]] conspired to destroy the Jedi Tower and bring an end to the Mandalorian occupation. They launched a [[Raid on the Jedi Tower|raid]] into the Tower's foundations and were able to plant demolition charges, without detonating them. While planning to strike decisively against the tower and blow it up with a [[MM-40 thermal charge|MM-40 Thermal Charge]], the Resistance planned to evacuate the surrounding areas and remove the possibility of civilians being killed. To make sure that Fett would still be in the Tower by the time they would be ready to destroy the structure, Resistance leader [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Haydel Goravvus]] suggested that a reconnaissance team investigate the Jedi Tower beforehand. Another member of the team would also have to access a hatch within the Tower in order to allow explosives to be planted at the base of the structure. Zayne Carrick, who was aiding the Resistance, volunteered for the job, as well as [[Shel Jelavan]], sister of the slain Padawan Shad. Accompanying them was Jedi Master Raana Tey, who was maintaining the pretense that Carrick had killed Shad and the other Padawans. [[File:Gryphtowerexplody.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Hierogryph detonates the explosives and destroys the Jedi Tower.]] The day following their planning, the Resistance [[Mission to the Jedi Tower|enacted]] their plan. Unbeknownst to them, Fett and his command staff had already evacuated the building, and only the tactical equipment remained to be packed up. The task was assigned to the Mandalorian [[Gormer]]. Within an [[Time/Legends|hour]], Tey infiltrated the Tower through a ventilation shaft, and Carrick and Jelavan were able to sneak in via the skybridge disguised as a Mandalorian and a [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]], respectively. Carrick made his way to the former Council chambers, discovering that only Gormer remained. Tey rushed in and [[Sai tok/Legends|cut down]] the Mandalorian. Intent on killing Carrick, Tey lunged at him, commencing a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]]. Meanwhile, the Resistance members in the foundation of the Tower received the news that Fett had attacked their base and that they were to fall back to a [[The Pit (Taris)|safe haven]]. Assisting [[Gadon Thek]]—the leader of a [[Hidden Beks|swoop gang]] that had joined forces with the Resistance—at the foundation, Hierogryph insisted that Jelavan and Carrick would need extraction from the Tower, and Thek reluctantly accepted, taking the Snivvian on his [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike. By the time Hierogryph and Thek arrived, Tey had been [[Shiak|stabbed]] in the [[Stomach/Legends|stomach]] by Jelavan, who had intervened between the Jedi Master and Carrick after realizing that the young man had not in fact killed her brother. Hierogryph and Thek recovered Carrick and Jelavan through the skylight, which had been shattered in the duel, but Tey recovered and leaped toward her opponent. As she was caught on a pane of transparisteel, Carrick offered to help her. However, as Tey drew her lightsaber in order to cut her hand free from the pane, Hierogryph misinterpreted the move as a hostile one, immediately activating the detonator and setting off the explosives laid within the Tower's foundation. Tey was killed as the Jedi Tower collapsed, and the other Resistance members escaped, largely unharmed. ===Reclamation=== Following the ruination of Taris after [[Darth Malak]] ordered the surface [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|destroyed via bombardment from orbit]], the ruins of the Tower were buried under the weight of the rest of the city. Located in a region known as the [[Sinking City]], the tower's remains were located by members of the Jedi Order during reclamation efforts initiated by the Republic. The Order stationed several Jedi at the site to excavate the ruins and locate any lost relics or artifacts which could be salvaged and shipped to the Order's [[Jedi Temple (Tython)/Legends|new home]] on [[Tython/Legends|Tython]].''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Kotor_tower_side-JJM.png|thumb|right|175px|Some of John Jackson Miller's schematics for the Tower]] The Jedi Tower first appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]], the opening issue of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series written by [[John Jackson Miller]]. The Tower served as the location for the initiation of the main plot of the series, and the penciler for ''Commencement, Part 1'', [[Brian Ching]], even managed to include it in the very first panel.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor01.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #1''}} The number of walkways joining the main tower and the smaller, vertical tower varies between the appearances in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Commencement|''Commencement'']] story arc penciled by Ching, and the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Knights of Suffering|''Knights of Suffering'']] story arc penciled by [[Dustin Weaver]]. The former clearly shows only four walkways, whereas in the latter, the Tower has five. While writing Commencement, Miller sent rough schematics of the Tower to Ching due to the fact that every detail of the Tower had to be situated with the action scenes in the story arc in mind.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor06.html|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #6}} Miller took a careful approach to writing [[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of Suffering, Part 2'']] with regards to the destruction of the Tower, and stated on his website that ''"after [[Wikipedia:September 11 attacks|September 11, 2001]], [he] felt the proposed destruction of a skyscraper — even a fictional one during wartime — was not something [he] should approach lightly."'' He felt that Carrick's concern for civilians in the vicinity of the Tower was an appropriate reaction for a Jedi.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor23.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #23''}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Jedi Tower}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 0|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 0]] *{{KOTORbackups|0}} {{Imo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 1|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 2|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 2]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 3]] {{Mo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] *{{KOTORbackups|15}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]] {{Vision|[[Raana Tey]]}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]] {{Mo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 26|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 26]] {{Mo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 33|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 33]] {{Imo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 34|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 34]] {{Mo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 40|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 40]] {{Vision|[[Jarael]]}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' {{C|Under ''Zayne Carrick''}} *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content={{Reflist}}}} {{Interlang |de=Jedi-Turm#legends |es=Torre Jedi |pl=Tarisiańska Akademia Jedi }} [[Category:Jedi academies]] [[Category:Upper City locations]]
